title: LenderDB Crawler Extraction Schema
description: Defines every field the crawler must attempt to extract, where to look, and how to normalize the result.

name.description: Legal or trade name of the lending entity.

look_for: H1 on homepage or 'About Us' page
extraction.normalize: Title case. Strip 'LLC', 'Inc', 'Corp', 'NA', 'FSB' suffixes only when a short-form name exists as an alias.

shortName.description: Common abbreviated name brokers use in conversation.

look_for: How the company refers to itself in press releases

extraction.fallback: If regulatory license exists = bank or credit_union. If tied to manufacturer = captive. Otherwise = specialty_finance.

tier.description: Market tier based on deal size capacity and national footprint.

derive_from_rules: Tier 1: maxDealSize > $50M AND countriesServed has 3+ countries
Tier 2: maxDealSize $5M-$50M AND national US or 2+ countries
extraction.note: Do not extract directly — compute from deal size and geographic footprint after those fields are extracted.

parentCompany.description: Parent corporation if the lender is a subsidiary or division.

look_for: Google search first result for lender name + 'equipment finance'
extraction.normalize: Always store as https://www.domain.com — no trailing slash, no subpaths.

hqCountry.description: ISO 3166-1 alpha-2 country code for headquarters.

extraction.normalize: Map to ISO 2-letter code: United States → US, Canada → CA, United Kingdom → GB, Australia → AU, Germany → DE, France → FR, Netherlands → NL.

hqState.description: US state abbreviation (2 letters) for US-headquartered lenders.

look_for: Same as hqCountry — extract state from address

minDealSize.description: Minimum transaction size the lender will consider, in USD.

look_for: Application minimum note ('Deals under $X not accepted')
extraction.normalize: Convert all to USD integer. '$5k' → 5000, '$1.5M' → 1500000, '$2 million' → 2000000.
extraction.fallback: If not found, set to null and flag for manual review. Do NOT guess.

maxDealSize.description: Maximum single transaction size the lender will consider, in USD.

look_for: Regulatory capital ratio filings can imply limits
extraction.normalize: Same as minDealSize — convert to USD integer.
extraction.note: If page says 'no maximum', set to 500000000 (500M) as sentinel.

preferredDealMin.description: Lower bound of the lender's preferred deal sweet spot (not hard minimum).

look_for: 'Sweet spot', 'typically finance', 'most comfortable with', 'focus on deals between'
Sales rep quotes in trade press
extraction.note: Softer signal than minDealSize. Use only when explicitly stated or strongly implied.

preferredDealMax.description: Upper bound of the lender's preferred deal sweet spot.

industriesFavored.description: Industries the lender actively targets or promotes as focus areas.

look_for: 'Industries We Serve' or 'Markets We Serve' page sections
Navigation menu items that represent industry verticals
Homepage hero copy ('financing for healthcare, construction, transportation')
Equipment categories imply industries (medical = healthcare, excavators = construction)
extraction.normalize: Map extracted phrases to taxonomy values. 'Medical' → healthcare, 'Trucking' → transportation, 'Solar/Wind' → clean_energy, 'Restaurants' → food_service.

industriesAvoided.description: Industries the lender explicitly will not finance.

look_for: Underwriting guidelines (often in broker program PDF)

equipmentCategories.description: Types of equipment the lender finances.

look_for: Equipment type listings on product pages
Photo content (what equipment appears in imagery)
Case studies ('financed a fleet of...', 'funded the purchase of...')
extraction.normalize: Consolidate. 'MRI machines, surgical robots' → medical. 'Excavators, bulldozers' → heavy_equipment.

countriesServed.description: Countries where the lender actively originates deals. ISO alpha-2 codes.

look_for: International or global mention on homepage
Regulatory licenses (each country requires separate registration)
extraction.normalize: ISO 2-letter codes. If only US addresses found, set ['US']. If 'North America' mentioned, set ['US', 'CA'].

statesServed.description: US state restrictions for lenders with limited geographic footprint.

look_for: State licensing disclosures (footer or legal page)
'We currently serve customers in the following states:'
State-specific regulatory filings (NMLS for non-bank lenders)
Application form state dropdown with limited options
extraction.note: Only populate if lender explicitly restricts by state. Leave empty if national coverage.

riskTolerance.description: How the lender approaches credit risk on borrowers.

derive_from: Explicit language: 'investment-grade only', 'strong financials required' = conservative

minCreditFico.description: Minimum personal FICO score the lender typically requires.

look_for: FAQ ('What credit score do I need?')
FAQ ('How long must my business be open?')
extraction.normalize: Convert to months. '2 years' → 24, '6 months' → 6, '1 year' → 12.

approvalSpeed.description: Typical time from application to credit decision.

look_for: Case studies or testimonials mentioning speed
extraction.note: Prioritize broker-program-specific turnaround times over general marketing claims.

appetiteSignals.description: Dynamic intelligence snippets about current lender appetite. The most valuable field.

items.text: Plain English statement about what the lender is actively doing right now.

look_for: Press releases (new product launches, geographic expansions)
Industry conference session descriptions (often reveal strategic priorities)
Trade publication articles mentioning the lender
Broker network community posts about lender behavior
Regulatory filings describing portfolio growth or contraction
positive_signals: Launching a new industry vertical program
Announcing increased deal capacity or credit facility
Partnering with vendors in a new sector
Press release about record volume in an industry
caution_signals: Raising minimum credit scores or deal sizes
Exiting a geography or product line
Leadership changes in credit or originations
Portfolio concentration warnings in trade press
negative_signals: Announcing layoffs in originations or credit
Acquired by or merging with larger entity (appetite uncertain)
extraction.text_format: One to two sentences, factual, present tense. Include the specific industry, geography, or product mentioned. Example: 'Actively expanding healthcare portfolio Q1 2026 — medical imaging and diagnostic equipment prioritized.'
extraction.note: Signals older than 180 days should be discarded unless no newer signals exist.

originationsContact.description: Name or title of the broker-facing originations contact.

look_for: LinkedIn: search for '[lender name] business development' or 'originations'
extraction.normalize: Prefer job title over personal name to avoid staleness. 'Equipment Finance Desk', 'Broker Relations', 'National Accounts'.

originationsEmail.description: Email address for broker deal submissions.

look_for: Same as originationsEmail — prefer broker-specific line over general customer service
extraction.normalize: Store as plain digits with country code: +12125551234. Strip extensions unless critical.

brokerFriendly.description: Whether the lender actively works with independent brokers.

true_signals: Has a 'Broker Program' or 'Partner Program' page
Has a broker portal or deal submission form
Attending NAELB or equipment broker conferences
false_signals: Direct-only language ('We work directly with businesses')
No broker or partner mention anywhere on site

brokerRegistrationRequired.description: Whether brokers must register/onboard before submitting deals.

false_signals: Deal submission form accessible without login

confidenceScore.description: Crawler-assigned confidence in the accuracy of this record.

extraction.compute_as: Weighted average across all extracted fields.

adjustments: +20 if data verified from lender's own website
-10 if any critical field is inferred (not directly stated)
-5 per field marked as approximate or inferred

extraction.note: Cap at 95 — never 100 (data can always be stale).

lastVerifiedDate.description: ISO 8601 timestamp of the most recent crawl/verification.

extraction.set_to: Crawler run timestamp when record is written or updated.
extraction.note: Update this field on every successful crawl, even if no data changed.

sources.description: All sources used to build this record, with crawl timestamps.

items.name: Human-readable source name
items.url: Full URL of the source page
items.sourceType: association | regulatory | marketplace | vendor | conference | direct | broker_network

extraction.note: Every field extracted must be traceable to at least one entry here. This is required for AWS Data Exchange audit compliance.

validation_rules: minDealSize must be less than maxDealSize
preferredDealMin must be >= minDealSize if set
preferredDealMax must be <= maxDealSize if set
sources array must have at least 1 entry
countriesServed must have at least 1 entry
lendingProducts must have at least 1 entry
industriesFavored must have at least 1 entry
appetiteSignals.type must be one of: positive, caution, negative
All date fields must be valid ISO 8601
